Package microui.core.style

Class GradientColor

java.lang.Object
microui.core.style.AbstractColor
microui.core.style.AbstractGradientColor
microui.core.style.GradientColor
public final class GradientColor extends AbstractGradientColor
A gradient color that dynamically transitions between two colors based on a boolean condition. Extends AbstractGradientColor to provide conditional animation control where the gradient progresses based on an external condition rather than automatic timing.

The GradientColor animates between start and end colors when a specified condition is true, and reverses the animation when the condition is false. This is useful for creating interactive color effects that respond to user input or application state.

Status: STABLE - Do not modify Last Reviewed: 11.10.2025

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • GradientColor

      public GradientColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end, BooleanSupplier condition)
      Constructs a GradientColor with specified start and end colors and a condition.
      Parameters:
      start - the starting color (cannot be null)
      end - the ending color (cannot be null)
      condition - the condition supplier that controls animation direction (cannot be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any parameter is null

  • Method Details

    • resetAnimationProgress

      public void resetAnimationProgress()
      Resets the animation progress to the beginning (0%). Useful for restarting the gradient animation from the start.
